task_completion.description: Set the observer location to Mytilene, Lesbos, Greece (39.11 N, 26.55 E, Altitude 10m).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium 'Location' window is open showing Latitude ~39.11 N (or 39°06' N), Longitude ~26.55 E (or 26°33' E), and Altitude 10m, OR the bottom status bar displays 'Mytilene' or the correct coordinates.

task_completion.description: Set the date to January 25, 570 BCE (year -569) and time to 22:15:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium 'Date and Time' window is open showing year -569, month 1, day 25, and time 22:15:00, OR the bottom status bar displays this specific date and time.

task_completion.description: Disable Atmosphere, enable Azimuthal Grid, and enable Constellation Art.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky view shows a dark background without blue atmospheric scattering, circular/radial lines of the azimuthal grid are visible on the sky, and illustrations of mythological figures (constellation art) are drawn over the stars. The corresponding icons on the bottom toolbar may also be highlighted/toggled.

task_completion.description: Center the view on the Pleiades (M45) star cluster.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Pleiades star cluster (M45) is positioned in the center of the screen. There should be a selection reticle around it and information text in the top-left corner identifying 'Pleiades' or 'M45'.

task_completion.description: Save the location and display settings to the application configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium 'Configuration' window (Main tab) is visible, indicating the agent clicked the 'Save settings' button to persist the current state.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot of the simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A temporary on-screen notification in Stellarium stating a screenshot was saved, OR a file manager/terminal window showing a new image file in the ~/Pictures/stellarium directory.

task_completion.description: Write a lecture summary to ~/Desktop/sappho_lecture.txt including the required keywords.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano) or terminal window is open showing the file 'sappho_lecture.txt' on the Desktop, and the visible text contains the words 'Sappho', 'Lesbos', '570', and 'Pleiades'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to configure the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Stellarium application interface and its configuration windows (Location, Date/Time, View), rather than the agent exclusively using a terminal to edit the ~/.stellarium/config.ini file.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered and viewed the sky simulation with the requested settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main Stellarium sky view is visible with the grid, art, and Pleiades centered, proving the agent didn't just open the settings windows and immediately close the app.

integrity.description: The agent created the text file using standard OS tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal echo/cat command is visible creating the file, rather than a script magically generating the output without visible interaction.
